concept: Craft / Make with Skill / Artisanal Creation
description: To make something with learned skill and careful attention—the practiced hand shaping material into useful or beautiful form. 'Theko' implies accumulated knowledge, trained sensitivity, and the marriage of intention with capability that distinguishes craft from mere assembly.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'the' /θe/, the dental fricative of thoughtful precision—breath controlled, attention focused. The 'e' engages with material. Then 'ko' concludes with velar firmness, the made thing taking solid form.
grammatical_notes: 'mia noru theko.' (I craft a bowl.) Material takes the instrumental: 'mia roe wolea noru theko.' (I craft a bowl from wood.) The event-noun rule licenses 'welao theko' (fine craft). Compare 'kealo' (create) for bringing the new into being and 'lorima' (build) for structural assembly.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Craft unites beauty and function, the artisan's touch visible in the made thing.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Craft carries generations of accumulated skill—hands learning from hands, knowledge embodied in technique rather than text.
